title: Read N Lines with getline
prompt_md:
## Goal
Write a program that reads an integer N, then reads N full lines (which may contain spaces), and prints each line prefixed by its 1-based index and a colon.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 10).
- Lines 2..N+1: one line of text each (may contain spaces, length 1–50).

## Output
Print N lines, each in the format `I: text` where I is the 1-based line number.

Example — input `2\nhello world\nfoo bar` → output:
```
1: hello world
2: foo bar
```

## Requirements
1. Read N with `>>`, then use `std::cin.ignore()` to discard the newline, then use `std::getline` inside a loop.
2. Index starts at 1.
3. Hidden test uses N=1 with a single-word line and N=3 with mixed-space lines.

test_cases.label: Hidden: three mixed lines
test_cases.stdin:
3
alpha beta
gamma
delta epsilon zeta
test_cases.expected_stdout:
1: alpha beta
2: gamma
3: delta epsilon zeta

title: Token Counter — fix the bug
prompt_md:
## Goal
This program is supposed to read a count N, then read N words (single tokens, no spaces), and print how many of them are longer than 3 characters. It has one bug — find and fix it.

## Input
- Line 1: integer N (1 ≤ N ≤ 20).
- Lines 2..N+1: one word per line.

## Output
Print exactly one line: `Long words: K` where K is the count of words with length > 3.

## Requirements
1. The bug is a logic error — the program compiles cleanly.
2. Hidden tests include N=1 where the single word is exactly 3 characters (should not count it), and N=5 with a mix.

test_cases.label: Hidden: exactly 3-char word should not count
